evals.prompt: hey build the bottom composer for our agent client. ios 26 swiftui, liquid glass, attachments row, tools expand, send turns into stop while streaming, voice button too
evals.expected_output: A self-contained SwiftUI iOS 26 component with native glassEffect/GlassEffectContainer, stateful send/stop/voice/tools, accessibility considerations, and preview.
expectations: The output uses @available(iOS 26.0, *) or an equivalent iOS 26 availability gate.
The output uses GlassEffectContainer around related composer controls.
The output includes send/stop icon state change with contentTransition(.symbolEffect(.replace)).
The output includes Reduce Transparency or Reduce Motion handling.
The output includes a SwiftUI preview.

evals.prompt: audit this LiquidGlassKit dependency choice: should we ship DnV1eX or Theseus for our iOS 26 app? we only need native iOS 26 UI mostly
evals.expected_output: An audit-style recommendation that native APIs should be default, DnV1eX/Theseus are backport/shader inspiration, and private API/App Store risks are called out.
expectations: The output recommends native iOS 26 Apple APIs as the default.
The output classifies DnV1eX/LiquidGlassKit as shader inspiration or backport-only rather than a primary dependency.
The output classifies Theseus as backport/shader inspiration rather than native iOS 26 replacement.
The output warns against private APIs or internal UIKit class probing.
The output mentions App Store or maintenance risk.

evals.prompt: make me a floating glass toolbar over a pdf canvas. swiftui, phone first, annotate/share/page controls, please don't make the whole pdf frosted
evals.expected_output: A SwiftUI iOS 26 media/canvas overlay implementation that puts glass only on controls, keeps content readable, includes preview and accessibility notes.
expectations: The output keeps the PDF/media content itself non-glass or content-first.
The output uses glass buttons or glassEffect for overlay controls.
The output includes annotate and share controls.
The output uses phone-first safe-area or edge placement.
The output includes a SwiftUI preview.

evals.prompt: our SwiftUI tab app needs native iOS 26 search, a compact active-agent strip above the tab bar, and the tab bar should get out of the way when scrolling. don't build a fake tab bar unless needed.
evals.expected_output: A native TabView/search/accessory implementation that uses searchable, tabViewBottomAccessory, tabBarMinimizeBehavior, and avoids unnecessary custom tab recreation.
expectations: The output uses native TabView rather than a custom tab recreation as the first choice.
The output includes searchable or a search-role tab/search guidance.
The output uses tabViewBottomAccessory or clearly describes the UIKit bottom accessory equivalent.
The output uses tabBarMinimizeBehavior or explains why it is not appropriate.
The output includes a SwiftUI preview.

evals.prompt: i have an iPad split view with a generated image/canvas on the detail side and a right inspector. make the visual backdrop continue under the inspector and add a bottom liquid glass ask-about-this-canvas composer. ios 26 only.
evals.expected_output: A native iOS 26 SwiftUI implementation using backgroundExtensionEffect for the media backdrop and safeAreaBar or safeAreaInset for the bottom composer, with glass controls but readable content.
expectations: The output uses or explicitly recommends backgroundExtensionEffect for the media/image backdrop under sidebar or inspector chrome.
The output uses safeAreaBar or safeAreaInset for the bottom composer instead of manual keyboard-overlay positioning.
The output keeps Liquid Glass on controls/overlays rather than applying glass to the entire content body.
The output includes @available(iOS 26.0, *) or an equivalent availability guard.
